selection_rule: Only keep platforms with a public GitHub repository and a top-tier conference or journal publication. Concept notes, closed demos, consumer products, and papers without runnable code stay in the watchlist.
criteria: public GitHub repository or official open-source repo
accepted paper in a top conference/journal or a strong robotics/AI venue
spatial intelligence relevance: sensing, scene state, embodied interaction, traffic/city simulation, or multi-agent behavior
clear architectural lesson for Tongyu Central Hub

platforms.name: Stanford Generative Agents / Smallville
platforms.paper: Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior
platforms.architecture: memory stream -> retrieval -> reflection -> planning -> action
platforms.tongyu_lesson: Build long-running space personas with memory, reflection, daily plan, and human natural-language intervention.

platforms.name: AI Habitat / Habitat-Sim
platforms.paper: Habitat: A Platform for Embodied AI Research
platforms.tongyu_lesson: Use scene graph, sensor configuration, and benchmark metrics as the model for spatial perception and evaluation.
platforms.bridge_payload: scene/task observations -> SensorFrame; benchmark events -> evaluation record

platforms.name: iGibson 2.0
platforms.paper: iGibson 2.0: Object-Centric Simulation for Robot Learning of Everyday Household Tasks
platforms.architecture: object state -> predicate logic -> interactive tasks -> robot actions
platforms.tongyu_lesson: Represent space objects with actionable states such as wet, hot, open, occupied, and dirty.

platforms.name: VirtualHome
platforms.paper: VirtualHome: Simulating Household Activities via Programs
platforms.architecture: activity program -> evolving graph -> Unity simulation
platforms.tongyu_lesson: Translate human routines into executable spatial programs and evolving state graphs.

platforms.name: CARLA
platforms.paper: CARLA: An Open Urban Driving Simulator
platforms.architecture: urban map -> sensors -> actors -> traffic scenarios
platforms.tongyu_lesson: Use a city-scale sensor/actor pipeline and scenario runner as the reference for site-scale simulation bridges.
platforms.bridge_payload: traffic state + weather + pedestrian/vehicle counts -> SensorFrame

platforms.name: SMARTS
platforms.paper: SMARTS: An Open-Source Scalable Multi-Agent RL Training School for Autonomous Driving
platforms.architecture: scenario zoo -> multi-agent policies -> interaction metrics
platforms.tongyu_lesson: Borrow multi-agent scenario orchestration and interaction metrics for crowd/robot coordination.
platforms.bridge_payload: agent interaction metrics -> negotiation proposal + risk tags

platforms.name: MetaDrive
platforms.paper: MetaDrive: Composing Diverse Driving Scenarios for Generalizable Reinforcement Learning
platforms.architecture: procedural map -> traffic setting -> lightweight simulator
platforms.tongyu_lesson: Use compositional scenario generation to stress-test Tongyu policies before field deployment.
platforms.bridge_payload: scenario parameters + safety metrics -> SensorFrame + evaluation record

platforms.name: CityFlow
platforms.paper: CityFlow: A Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning Environment for Large Scale City Traffic Scenario
platforms.architecture: road network + traffic flow -> microscopic simulator -> Python RL API
platforms.tongyu_lesson: Use fast microscopic traffic simulation as a proxy for crowd-flow and path-guidance experiments.
platforms.bridge_payload: traffic flow / queue / intersection load -> SensorFrame

watchlist.name: PlanGPT / tailored urban planning agent
watchlist.reason: ACL 2025 Industry paper is relevant to urban planning agents, but a public runnable GitHub repository was not confirmed in this pass.
watchlist.use: Keep as planning-agent literature; do not present as platform benchmark until code is public.

watchlist.name: CityVerse / urban agent benchmark
watchlist.reason: Promising urban data/API/benchmark direction, but it should stay in watchlist until venue status and runnable code maturity are confirmed.
watchlist.use: Monitor for urban-task taxonomy and evaluation metrics.

watchlist.name: EmbodiedCity / CityEQA
watchlist.reason: Relevant to open-city embodied question answering, but not selected as core benchmark under the strict code + top-venue gate.
watchlist.use: Use as inspiration for city-scale perception questions, not as a platform pillar.

watchlist.name: MIT CityScope
watchlist.reason: Strong participatory urban-interface idea, but not a clean reproducible GitHub platform under the current selection rule.
watchlist.use: Keep as interaction-method reference, not as core platform benchmark.

excluded.name: Apple spatial/consumer product line
excluded.reason: Consumer hardware/product ecosystem, not an open research platform for our central-hub architecture.

excluded.name: Concept-only urban AI systems
excluded.reason: No runnable repo or no confirmed top-venue paper; not suitable for the main comparison table.
